title: Unchecked Call Return Value in ERC20 transferFrom
reason: Not a practical issue here: the reserve buy path uses fixed cDAI/DAI integrations rather than arbitrary ERC20s, so generic non-standard token compatibility does not create an exploitable protocol vulnerability.

title: Missing Access Control on onDistribution Function
reason: Public triggering is explicitly intended by the contract comments. Standalone timing control is not reportable by itself; the real issue is the merged zero-slippage fee sale that public callers can trigger.

title: Unlimited Token Approvals Set by Anyone
reason: `setAddresses()` only refreshes approvals to addresses resolved from `NameService`, which is avatar-controlled. The public refresh function alone does not let an attacker redirect approvals to themselves.

title: Division by Zero in GoodMarketMaker.calculateMintInterest
reason: The reported path depends on privileged misconfiguration or an undeveloped reserve-depletion scenario. The agent did not demonstrate a concrete, realistic permissionless exploit from the current code.

title: Missing Input Validation in GoodMarketMaker.initializeToken
reason: This is a governance-parameter sanity concern, not a demonstrated exploit path or user-reachable vulnerability.

title: Potential Integer Overflow in BancorFormula
reason: Too speculative. Solidity 0.8 has checked arithmetic, and no concrete overflow-triggering input path or profit mechanism was shown.

title: Missing Pausable in DistributionHelper
reason: Absence of an emergency pause is a design preference, not a concrete security bug in itself.

title: Unchecked cDai Redeem and Mint Return Values
reason: The contract does check the Compound return codes and reverts on failure. Generic error strings are not a security issue.

title: Missing Zero Address Validation in setDistributionHelper
reason: This is a recoverable avatar misconfiguration, not an exploitable vulnerability path.

title: Missing Deadline Check in Uniswap Swaps
reason: Using `block.timestamp` as a same-transaction deadline is standard router usage and does not trap user funds in this flow.

title: Hardcoded Oracle Address in DistributionHelper
reason: This is a maintainability/centralization concern rather than a concrete exploit that causes realistic protocol harm by itself.

title: Missing Validation of Array Length in collectInterest
reason: Duplicate staking addresses were not shown to create double-crediting, theft, or a realistic protocol-level failure from the current logic.

title: Inconsistent NonReentrant Implementation
reason: Implementation style only. The presence of custom reentrancy guards instead of OpenZeppelin's library is not a reportable vulnerability.
